RICHARDS, WATSON & GERSHON
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

DEC 2: i i :::11 '92

GLENN R. WATSON
RICHARD G. SEVERIN
DAVID M. GERSHON
DOUGLAS W. ARGUE

AMIRA T. ASAMURA
DANIEL N. LINS
TAYLOR L. FITZMAURICE
ROBERT D. FELDMAN
KAYSER C. BLUM
JOE F. MOORE
CRAIG A. STEELE
ROBERT M. MARLOVITZ
FRANK J. KORZEC
KURTISS A. GROSSMAN
DAWN R. ANDREWS

RICHARD RICHARDS
(1918-1982)

THIRTY-EIGHTH FLOOR
333 SOUTH HOPE STREET
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90071-1489
(213) 626-8484
(213) 629-0078

OF COUNSEL
RICHARD H. DANIEL

1681086

OUR FILE NUMBER

L5115-00109

December 18, 1992

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Chief Legal Counsel
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 "J" Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Reference:    Request for Formal Written Advice on Behalf of
              City of La Habra Heights Mayor Richard N. Newbre

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     I have been asked by the Mayor of the City of La Habra
Heights, Richard N. Newbre, to obtain formal written advice
regarding possible conflicts of interest concerning his
participation in decisions regarding the Powder Canyon Specific
Plan and related land use decisions and approvals.

Background

     Forum Country Clubs of California (hereinafter "Forum")
is a real estate development firm which has submitted
applications to the city of La Habra Heights for development of
an 18 hole golf course and 150 single family residential lots on
a site of approximately 540 acres in the north-eastern foothills
of the City. The proposed project is known as the "Powder Canyon
Country Club" and is subject to several discretionary approvals
required to be made by the City Council, including a specific
plan, subdivision map and a development agreement.

     Richard N. Newbre is a member of the City Council and
currently chairs the City Council as Mayor. He resides on a 27
acre parcel of land located off a cul-de-sac named Canada Sombre
Road in the City of La Habra Heights. His property is

RICHARDS, WATSON & GERSHON

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
December 18, 1992
Page 2

irregularly shaped with a small portion of the property being as
close as 350 feet from the boundaries of the Forum Project. His
residential structure is located at least 1,000 feet from the
boundaries of the Forum Project. A narrow 25 acre site lies
between his property and the Forum site. For purposes of
considering the applicability of FPPC regulation 18703.1, we are
informed by City staff that Mayor Newbre's 27 acre parcel is
larger than 125 percent of the median residential lot size in the
City.

We are informed by City staff that the project is not
anticipated to be visible from his residence and will not affect
his view or privacy because of the parcel of land and hilly
topography that separates his property from the Forum
project. Mayor Newbre currently keeps horses on his property and
he has used equestrian trails that traverse the Forum project
property for horseback riding purposes. The Forum project may
eliminate some of these existing equestrian trails. The project
is not anticipated to create additional traffic down arterial
streets in his neighborhood because the project will be served by
streets located at the other end of the project away from Mayor
Newbre's immediate neighborhood.

An issue that may be decided during the Forum project
approvals is whether Hart Ranch Road will be designed so that it
can ultimately be extended to connect as a through street with
Hacienda Boulevard. I am informed by staff, that the extension
of Hart Ranch Road is not required to mitigate traffic impacts of
the project, but is being considered as a circulation design
preference. If this design is approved, the eventual extension
of Hart Ranch Road will traverse the northeasterly tip of Mayor
Newbre's property over a recorded easement for such purpose.
Therefore, the extension, if approved, will not require
acquisition of property from Mayor Newbre. The portion of Mayor
Newbre's property that could be affected by this road extension
is in a hillside portion of Mayor Newbre's property at the
opposite end of where Mayor Newbre's home and horse stables are
located. There are no current plans to either require Forum to
construct the extension or to have the City or some other entity
construct the extension.

Questions

1)     May Mayor Newbre participate in decisions of the City
Council to extend or not extend Hart Ranch Road?

2)     May Mayor Newbre participate in decisions regarding the
Powder Canyon Specific Plan, development agreement and related
land use approvals?

RICHARDS, WATSON & GERSHON

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
December 18, 1992
Page 3

3) If Mayor Newbre has a conflict of interest in one or more of the decisions, may the City bifurcate the decisions so as to permit Mayor Newbre to participate in some of the decisions? For example, if Mayor Newbre has a conflict of interest in the decision relating to the extension of Hart Ranch Road, can the City Council address that issue first with Mayor Newbre's abstention and then permit Mayor Newbre to participate in the rest of the decisions relating to the project?

Thank you for your assistance in this matter. Please feel free to contact Kevin G. Ennis of our office at your convenience if you need further clarification of the questions or if you desire any additional background information.

Very truly yours,

Carol W. Lynch
City Attorney

CC: Mayor Newbre
CWL:KGE:pjr
1681086

sections.question:
1) May Mayor Newbre participate in decisions of the City Council to extend or not extend Hart Ranch Road?

2) May Mayor Newbre participate in decisions regarding the Powder Canyon Specific Plan, development agreement and related land use approvals?

3) If Mayor Newbre has a conflict of interest in one or more of the decisions, may the City bifurcate the decisions so as to permit Mayor Newbre to participate in some of the decisions? For example, if Mayor Newbre has a conflict of interest in the decision relating to the extension of Hart Ranch Road, can the City Council address that issue first with Mayor Newbre's abstention and then permit Mayor Newbre to participate in the rest of the decisions relating to the project?

Thank you for your assistance in this matter. Please feel free to contact Kevin G. Ennis of our office at your convenience if you need further clarification of the questions or if you desire any additional background information.
sections.facts:
information provided includes:

- Forum Country Clubs of California (hereinafter "Forum") is a real estate development firm which has submitted applications to the City of La Habra Heights for development of an 18 hole golf course and 150 single family residential lots on a site of approximately 540 acres in the north-eastern foothills of the City.
- The proposed project is known as the "Powder Canyon Country Club" and is subject to several discretionary approvals required to be made by the City Council, including a specific plan, subdivision map and a development agreement.
- Richard N. Newbre is a member of the City Council and currently chairs the City Council as Mayor. He resides on a 27 acre parcel of land located off a cul-de-sac named Canada Sombre Road in the City of La Habra Heights. His property is

irregularly shaped with a small portion of the property being as close as 350 feet from the boundaries of the Forum Project. His residential structure is located at least 1,000 feet from the boundaries of the Forum Project. A narrow 25 acre site lies between his property and the Forum site. For purposes of considering the applicability of FPPC regulation 18703.1, we are informed by City staff that Mayor Newbre's 27 acre parcel is larger than 125 percent of the median residential lot size in the City.

We are informed by City staff that the project is not anticipated to be visible from his residence and will not affect his view or privacy because of the parcel of land and hilly topography that separates Mayor Newbre's property from the Forum project. Mayor Newbre currently keeps horses on his property and he has used equestrian trails that traverse the Forum project property for horseback riding purposes. The Forum project may eliminate some of these existing equestrian trails. The project is not anticipated to create additional traffic down arterial streets in his neighborhood because the project will be served by streets located at the other end of the project away from Mayor Newbre's immediate neighborhood.

An issue that may be decided during the Forum project approvals is whether Hart Ranch Road will be designed so that it can ultimately be extended to connect as a through street with Hacienda Boulevard. I am informed by staff, that the extension of Hart Ranch Road is not required to mitigate traffic impacts of the project, but is being considered as a circulation design preference. If this design is approved, the eventual extension of Hart Ranch Road will traverse the northeasterly tip of Mayor Newbre's property over a recorded easement for such purpose. Therefore, the extension, if approved, will not require acquisition of property from Mayor Newbre. The portion of Mayor Newbre's property that could be affected by this road extension is in a hillside portion of Mayor Newbre's property at the opposite end of where Mayor Newbre's home and horse stables are located. There are no current plans to either require Forum to construct the extension or to have the City or some other entity construct the extension.

QUESTION: 1) May Mayor Newbre participate in decisions of the City Council to extend or not extend Hart Ranch Road?

2) May Mayor Newbre participate in decisions regarding the Powder Canyon Specific Plan, development agreement and related land use approvals?

3) If Mayor Newbre has a conflict of interest in one or more of the decisions, may the City bifurcate the decisions so as to permit Mayor Newbre to participate in some of the decisions? For example, if Mayor Newbre has a conflict of interest in the decision relating to the extension of Hart Ranch Road, can the City Council address that issue first with Mayor Newbre's abstention and then permit Mayor Newbre to participate in the rest of the decisions relating to the project?

Thank you for your assistance in this matter. Please feel free to contact Kevin G. Ennis of our office at your convenience if you need further clarification of the questions or if you desire any additional background information.
